touches.ai_summary: Cold call connected with Yuki King, Head of RevOps at BirchSync. Yuki expressed interest in automation solutions for their onboarding process, which currently involves significant manual work across their 1000-person PropTech organization. Call was brief but productive, with Yuki agreeing to a follow-up discussion.

questions_asked.question: How are you currently handling onboarding for new properties in your system?
questions_asked.answer: We have a mix of manual processes and some legacy tools. It's taking our team about 2-3 weeks per property, which is becoming a bottleneck as we scale.

questions_asked.question: What kind of automation capabilities does your platform offer specifically for PropTech workflows?
questions_asked.answer: We offer configurable workflow automation with data validation, document processing, and integration with major property management systems. I can show you examples in a demo.

next_steps: Yuki to review calendar and propose times for discovery call
Rep to send overview deck and case study from similar PropTech company

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with overview materials and PropTech case study. Yuki responded positively, confirming interest in a deeper discovery call and mentioning that Jordan Murphy from Sales would likely need to be involved for procurement.

questions_asked.question: Can you share the case study you mentioned from the other PropTech company?
questions_asked.answer: Attached - it's a mid-market real estate platform that reduced onboarding time by 60% using our automations.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next week
Yuki to brief Jordan Murphy on initial conversation

touches.ai_summary: Email exchange confirming discovery call scheduled. Yuki shared that they're evaluating automation as a strategic priority for Q2 and have budget allocated. Mentioned their sales team of ~25 people would be primary users.

questions_asked.question: How many team members would be using the platform initially?
questions_asked.answer: Probably around 23-25 across our sales and ops teams - anyone touching the onboarding workflow.

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for Thursday 2pm
Yuki to prepare current workflow documentation

touches.ai_summary: Post-discovery call follow-up. Rep summarized key pain points discussed: manual data entry errors, lack of visibility into onboarding status, and inability to scale current process. Yuki confirmed accuracy and expressed strong interest in seeing a demo tailored to their workflow.

questions_asked.question: How quickly could we implement this if we move forward?
questions_asked.answer: Typical implementation for your use case is 3-4 weeks, including training and data migration.

next_steps: Schedule demo for following week
Rep to prepare custom demo environment with BirchSync branding
Yuki to invite Jordan Murphy to demo

touches.ai_summary: Demo follow-up email. Yuki responded enthusiastically about the automation capabilities shown, particularly the document processing and integration features. Requested pricing information for 23 seats with consulting package to help with implementation.

questions_asked.question: What's included in the consulting package vs the standard implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Consulting includes dedicated solutions architect, custom workflow design, and 40 hours of hands-on configuration support vs 10 hours in standard.

next_steps: Rep to prepare formal proposal with pricing
Yuki to discuss internally with Jordan and finance

touches.ai_summary: Brief exec meeting with Yuki to present formal proposal. Reviewed $20,300 ACV for 23 seats with automations plus consulting package. Yuki seemed aligned on value but mentioned need to run through procurement process with Jordan Murphy. No executive sponsor identified yet.

questions_asked.question: What's your typical contract length for this type of engagement?
questions_asked.answer: Standard is 12 months, but we offer some flexibility depending on commitment level.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to sign off on a purchase of this size at BirchSync?
questions_asked.answer: Jordan handles procurement, and I'll need to loop in our CFO for final approval, but I can champion this internally.

next_steps: Yuki to share proposal with Jordan and finance
Rep to follow up in 3-4 days
Yuki to identify timeline for internal review

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking on internal review progress. Yuki confirmed Jordan is reviewing and has some questions about contract terms and payment structure. CFO hasn't been engaged yet but Yuki is working on building business case.

questions_asked.question: Do you offer quarterly payment terms instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We can discuss payment terms - let me check with our finance team on quarterly options.

next_steps: Schedule call with Jordan Murphy to address procurement questions
Rep to provide payment terms options
Yuki to continue building internal business case

touches.ai_summary: Extended negotiation meeting with Yuki and Jordan Murphy joining. Discussed contract terms, payment structure, and implementation timeline. Jordan raised questions about commitment period and asked about month-to-month options after first year. Yuki remains supportive but acknowledged they still need exec approval from CFO. Some price sensitivity surfacing.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the 12-month commitment, especially given we're mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: We can potentially adjust start date or pro-rate, but annual commitment is standard for the consulting package tier.

questions_asked.question: What does support look like after the initial implementation period?
questions_asked.answer: Ongoing support is included - email/chat with 24hr response time, plus quarterly business reviews.

questions_asked.question: When are you hoping to have this implemented and running?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally by end of Q2, so we'd need to finalize in the next few weeks to hit that timeline.

objections: Annual commitment feels long given we're testing this for first time
Budget allocation may require some adjustments - CFO wants to see ROI projections
next_steps: Rep to provide ROI calculator and success metrics from similar customers
Jordan to review contract terms with legal
Yuki to schedule meeting with CFO to present business case
Rep to follow up on payment terms flexibility

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email providing ROI calculator and additional customer references. Checked in on CFO meeting timing. Yuki responded that they're still working to get CFO availability, and Jordan is in discussions with legal on contract language. Timeline may slip into early Q2 for implementation start.
next_steps: Await CFO meeting outcome
Jordan to provide feedback on contract terms from legal
Rep to check back in one week if no update
